item.title: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman exits Helion Energy's board as firms explore partnership
item.description: OpenAI Chief Executive Sam Altman said on Monday he has stepped down from the board of directors of Helion Energy, the fusion startup ​he has backed since 2015, as the companies start to explore ‌working together "at significant scale".

item.title: Ben & Jerry's Foundation joins lawsuit challenging The Magnum Ice Cream Company
item.description: The Ben & Jerry's Foundation, a non-profit funded by the ice cream brand, said on Monday it had won a court ​ruling to join a lawsuit challenging its ultimate parent company, ‌The Magnum Ice Cream Company , over contractual obligations and independence.

item.title: China's AliExpress tells EU lawmakers it is working to comply with law
item.description: Chinese online shopping site AliExpress said on Monday it was improving controls to comply with European Union regulations as European lawmakers grilled executives over ​sales of dangerous and counterfeit products in the bloc.

item.title: Risk-off trade keeps gold volatile as Iran war spooks investors
item.description: Acute volatility ​in gold prices is set to persist in the short term as investors cut risk, ‌with the Iran war boosting inflation fears, curbing bets on interest rate cuts, and weighing on the outlook for global growth, analysts said.

item.title: French Energy Ministry considers increasing oil refining capacity
item.description: The ‌French Energy Ministry has started evaluating the possibility of increasing the capacity of the country's five ​oil refineries in a bid to ​soften the impact of the conflict in Iran ⁠on energy prices, it said on ​Monday.

item.title: Madison Dearborn-backed defense contractor AEVEX files for US IPO
item.description: Defense technology prime ​contractor AEVEX, backed by ‌buyout firm Madison Dearborn Partners, on Monday filed for ​an initial public offering ​in the United States.

item.title: Apple to hold annual developers conference in June
item.description: Apple ​said ‌on Monday ​it ​would ⁠host ​its annual ​Worldwide Developers ​Conference ​online from ‌June ⁠8 to ​12.

item.title: AI may boost euro area productivity growth by 4% in 10 years, ECB says
item.description: Artificial intelligence could lift euro zone productivity growth by more than 4 percentage points over the next decade, although a ​prolonged energy shock could slow progress, European Central Bank chief ‌economist Philip Lane said on Monday.

item.title: Gilead nearing up to $2 billion buyout of biotech Ouro Medicines, FT reports
item.description: Gilead ​Sciences is nearing an ‌acquisition of Ouro Medicines, a biotech focused on immune disorder ​treatments, for up to $2 ​billion, the Financial Times reported ⁠on Monday.

item.title: Taco Bell, Dunkin’ franchisee to pay $1.5 mln in NYC scheduling case
item.description: A Taco Bell and Dunkin franchisee has agreed to pay more than $1.5 million to settle claims by New York City that its managers at two dozen restaurants ​violated a local law requiring fast food businesses to give workers advance notice ‌of their schedules and other protections, Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s office said on Monday.

item.title: Microsoft-backed startup raises $40 million for advanced chipmaking equipment tech
item.description: Lace, a Norway-headquartered chipmaking equipment startup which is backed by Microsoft, has raised $40 million in funding to ‌further develop a technology that could enable significant advances in semiconductor design and manufacturing, the company said on Monday.

item.title: CERAWEEK US, TotalEnergies to shift nearly $1 billion from wind to oil and gas
item.description: The United ​States and French energy major TotalEnergies said on Monday they ‌would redirect nearly $1 billion from offshore wind leases to U.S. oil and natural gas production.

item.title: UK considers expanding regulatory powers to tackle energy price gouging, profiteering
item.description: Britain's Prime Minister Keir Starmer said on ​Monday that his government ‌may consider expanding the powers of the competition regulator to ​tackle price gouging ​and profiteering in the energy ⁠market, given the impact ​of the Middle East conflict.

item.title: CERAWEEK US needs more energy development to power AI, Google president says
item.description: The U.S. may ​not be scaling ‌electricity fast enough for the expansion ​of artificial ​intelligence, Google's President and ⁠Chief Investment ​Officer Ruth Porat ​said on Monday, speaking about the vast ​amounts of ​power needed to scale ‌the ⁠company's AI data centers.

item.title: Exclusive: US SEC's ex-enforcement chief clashed with bosses before leaving, sources say
item.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission's top enforcement official, who left abruptly last week, had clashed with agency leaders over the direction of its enforcement program, including the handling of cases with ties to President ​Donald Trump and his family, according to three people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US construction spending unexpectedly falls in January
item.description: U.S. construction spending unexpectedly fell in January amid broad weakness in private projects, government data showed.

item.title: F1 owner Liberty Media can ride out Mideast conflict
item.description: The Middle East conflict has cut Formula One’s race calendar and battered the share price of its owner Liberty Media , but market analysts believe ​both will ride out the crisis with long-term prospects intact.

item.title: Alphabet's Wing to start drone delivery in San Francisco Bay Area
item.description: Alphabet-owned Wing plans to begin delivering packages by drone to homes in California's San Francisco Bay Area in ​the coming months, it said on Monday, extending its ‌rollout to one of its earliest testing grounds.

item.title: CERAWEEK Oil prices have not climbed enough to cause demand destruction, US Energy Secretary Chris Wright says
item.description: Global oil prices have ​not climbed enough ‌to cause demand destruction, U.S. Energy Secretary ​Chris Wright ​said on Monday at ⁠the CERAWeek energy ​conference in Houston ​Texas.

item.title: Fed's Miran still believes Fed should cut interest rates - Bloomberg TV
item.description: Federal Reserve Governor ​Stephen Miran said on Monday it’s premature to draw conclusions about how surging oil prices will affect the U.S. economy, as ‌he stuck to his guns and argued a softening jobs market requires more rate cuts from the central bank.

item.title: HSBC appoints David Rice as first chief AI officer
item.description: HSBC has appointed David Rice ​as its first ever ‌Chief AI Officer, the British bank said ​on Monday, as ​it seeks to cut costs ⁠by increasing the ​use of generative AI ​technology across its businesses worldwide.

item.title: Fed's Goolsbee: Inflation now the greater risk, watching expectations closely - CNBC
item.description: Inflation is the greater risk facing the U.S. ​economy right now with the ‌unemployment rate remaining fairly stable, Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee said on ​Monday, though a quick ​resolution of the Iran conflict could ⁠still leave the Fed poised ​to cut rates later this ​year.

item.title: DoorDash rolls out emergency relief program as surging pump prices hit gig workers
item.description: Food delivery company DoorDash said on ​Monday it is launching an ‌emergency relief program to help U.S. gig workers offset rising fuel costs.

item.title: Alibaba launches latest agentic AI platform with international unit's Accio Work
item.description: Alibaba has ​pushed further into the global race for agentic artificial intelligence, with its ‌international commerce division launching Accio Work, a plug‑and‑play “AI taskforce” it says can autonomously run complex business operations for small and medium-sized enterprises.

item.title: Nebius says "well-funded" for AI race after closing $4.3 billion debt raise
item.description: Nebius ​closed a $4.34 billion convertible debt funding round on Monday, with a ‌company executive saying that the European AI infrastructure firm is now "well-funded" to meet its 2026 capital spending plans of $16 billion to 20 billion.

item.title: OnlyFans owner Leonid Radvinsky dies of cancer at 43
item.description: Leonid Radvinsky, the owner of adult‑content ​platform OnlyFans, has died of cancer ‌at the age of 43, the company said in a statement on Monday.

item.title: China's open-source dominance threatens US AI lead, US advisory body warns
item.description: The dominance of China's open-source artificial intelligence is creating a "self-reinforcing competitive ​advantage", allowing it to challenge U.S. rivals despite restricted access to advanced AI chips, a U.S. congressional ‌advisory body said on Monday.

item.title: BlackRock CEO Fink backs staying invested amid volatility, flags AI shift
item.description: Asset management giant BlackRock's CEO Larry Fink urged clients to stay invested despite ongoing market volatility, and highlighted that artificial intelligence is reshaping ​investment strategies and the broader economy.

item.title: EU-Mercosur trade accord to apply provisionally from May 1
item.description: The European Union's free trade agreement ​with South American ‌bloc Mercosur will apply on a provisional basis from ​May 1, the ​European Commission said on ⁠Monday.

item.title: Instant view: World markets rally as Trump postpones military strikes on Iranian power plants
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said on Monday the U.S. has had good and productive conversations with Iran and he will order the military to postpone any military strikes against Iranian power plants and energy infrastructure.
